skill.name: backend-atomic-commit
skill.description: Guides strict backend pre-commit cleanup and atomic commit preparation for Django-style repositories. It checks local repo rules, runs configured quality gates, and proposes human-readable commit messages.
skill.summary: Prepare strict Django backend changes for clean, atomic commits.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, sensitive-data, and weak-cryptography patterns. Review confirms real medium-risk local command execution and hidden configuration access, but no malicious intent, exfiltration, prompt injection, or real weak cryptography was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Required for Quality Gates
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run git, Ruff, ty, Django checks, pytest, pre-commit, uv, and .security scripts. This is legitimate for a backend commit helper, but it can execute repository-controlled tools and hooks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly direct Bash-based execution of local development commands and scripts. The intent is quality validation, not malware, so the risk is elevated but contextually legitimate.

medium_findings.title: Hidden and Local Configuration File Access
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to read local rule files and inspect hidden configuration paths such as .pre-commit-config.yaml and .security scripts. This is expected for repository-specific validation, but it exposes the agent to untrusted local instructions and scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access is explicitly described and includes hidden repository paths. It appears necessary for the skill purpose, but these files may contain untrusted local behavior.

low_findings.title: Sensitive File and 1Password Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 1Password and .env references, but the skill uses them as safety guidance. It tells the agent to flag secrets and avoid staging secret-heavy files, not to read or exfiltrate them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The visible context is defensive secret-handling guidance. No network endpoint, secret reading instruction, or credential extraction behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic code in the reviewed skill. The cited content is natural-language guidance about commits, checks, migrations, and backend review behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic implementation. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated prose or command-like markdown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Command Execution Required for Quality Gates
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to run git, Ruff, ty, Django checks, pytest, pre-commit, uv, and .security scripts. This is legitimate for a backend commit helper, but it can execute repository-controlled tools and hooks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly direct Bash-based execution of local development commands and scripts. The intent is quality validation, not malware, so the risk is elevated but contextually legitimate.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden and Local Configuration File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the agent to read local rule files and inspect hidden configuration paths such as .pre-commit-config.yaml and .security scripts. This is expected for repository-specific validation, but it exposes the agent to untrusted local instructions and scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file access is explicitly described and includes hidden repository paths. It appears necessary for the skill purpose, but these files may contain untrusted local behavior.

content.value_statement: Backend commits often fail because local rules, linters, type checks, and tests are missed. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through strict pre-commit checks and atomic commit review.
actual_capabilities: Reads local backend guidance such as AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md when present.
Runs git status and diff commands to scope staged and unstaged changes.
Guides Ruff, ty, Django checks, pytest subsets, and pre-commit hooks.
Flags non-atomic staged changes before commit preparation.
Suggests concise ticket-prefixed commit messages without AI signatures.
Classifies remaining work with blocking, should-fix, and nit severity labels.
limitations: Requires a local repository with working developer tools installed.
May be too strict for small scripts or non-Django projects.
Does not guarantee that all production risks are covered by local checks.
Can modify files during pre-commit mode, so review diffs before committing.

use_cases.title: Clean backend changes before review
use_cases.description: Run formatting, linting, type checks, and local backend rules before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Validate staged changes for one commit
use_cases.description: Check whether staged files represent one coherent change and meet all required quality gates.

use_cases.title: Standardize team commit preparation
use_cases.description: Apply the same Django, security, and commit-message expectations across a backend team.

prompt_templates.title: Run a basic backend pre-commit pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backend-atomic-commit in pre-commit mode. Review my changed backend files, run available local checks, fix safe issues, and summarize remaining blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare staged files for an atomic commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backend-atomic-commit in atomic-commit mode. Check my staged files for atomicity, run required gates, and propose a ticket-prefixed commit message.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Django quality gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backend-atomic-commit for Django changes. Run Ruff, ty, Django checks, relevant pytest subsets, and configured security scripts for modified files only.

prompt_templates.title: Strictly audit risky backend changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backend-atomic-commit with strict review. Inspect migrations, logging, multi-tenant logic, local imports, and staged atomicity before declaring the commit ready.

output_examples.input: Pre-commit mode on modified Django files.
output: Fixes applied: formatting, import cleanup, and structured logging updates.
Checks run: Ruff passed, ty passed, Django check passed, targeted tests not available.
Remaining issues: one should-fix item for missing targeted test coverage.

output_examples.input: Atomic-commit mode on staged backend changes.
output: Verdict: not ready because staged files contain two unrelated changes.
Needs changes: split the schema update from the bugfix before committing.
Proposed commit: a concise ticket-prefixed subject for the first coherent change.

output_examples.input: Strict review of a risky migration change.
output: Needs changes: migration rollout risk detected for a non-nullable field on a hot table.
Recommendation: add nullable field first, backfill safely, then enforce the constraint later.

best_practices: Run it from the repository root so local configuration and security helpers are visible.
Review all file edits and tool output before creating the final commit.
Use atomic-commit mode only after staging the intended coherent change.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to approve unrelated staged changes as one commit.
Do not ignore failed quality gates in atomic-commit mode.
Do not run it on repositories where local scripts are not trusted.

faq.question: Does this skill create commits automatically?
faq.answer: No. It prepares and reviews changes, then proposes a commit message for the user to apply.

faq.question: Can it edit files?
faq.answer: Yes. In pre-commit mode it can apply safe formatting, linting, typing, and cleanup changes.

faq.question: Is it only for Django projects?
faq.answer: It is designed for Django-style backend repositories, but it can fall back to lighter Python checks.

faq.question: Why does it need Bash access?
faq.answer: It uses local git, lint, type-check, test, and pre-commit commands to verify repository state.

faq.question: Does it handle secrets?
faq.answer: It warns about secret-heavy files and hardcoded secrets. It should not read or expose secret values.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The skill declares support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
